characters.name: Kilgore Trout
characters.description: A prolific but largely unread science fiction writer. Vonnegut's most recurring character, appearing across seven novels.

characters.name: Eliot Rosewater
characters.description: Millionaire philanthropist and president of the Rosewater Foundation, who gives money to anyone who needs it.

characters.name: Howard W. Campbell Jr.
characters.description: An American playwright who becomes a Nazi propagandist while secretly working as a spy for the US government.

characters.name: Billy Pilgrim
characters.description: A WWII veteran and optometrist who has become 'unstuck in time,' experiencing his life in non-linear order.

characters.name: Montana Wildhack
characters.description: A movie star abducted by the Tralfamadorians and placed in the same zoo habitat as Billy Pilgrim.

characters.name: Valencia Merble
characters.description: Billy Pilgrim's devoted wife, who dies of carbon monoxide poisoning while rushing to visit Billy in hospital.

characters.name: Edgar Derby
characters.description: A high school teacher and POW who is executed by firing squad for looting a teapot from the ruins of Dresden.

characters.name: Paul Lazzaro
characters.description: A bitter, vengeful American POW who vows to have Billy Pilgrim killed after the war.

characters.name: Roland Weary
characters.description: An anti-tank gunner who blames Billy Pilgrim for his capture and death, igniting Lazzaro's vendetta.

characters.name: Dwayne Hoover
characters.description: A wealthy Pontiac dealer in Midland City who is going insane, in part after reading Kilgore Trout's novel.

characters.name: Francine Pefko
characters.description: Dwayne Hoover's secretary and mistress at the Pontiac dealership.

characters.name: Bunny Hoover
characters.description: Dwayne Hoover's gay son, who plays piano at the Holiday Inn lounge and is estranged from his father.

characters.name: Harry LeSabre
characters.description: Dwayne Hoover's sales manager, who hides a secret passion for cross-dressing.

characters.name: Rabo Karabekian
characters.description: A minimalist painter whose controversial painting at the Midland City arts festival sparks outrage.

characters.name: Helga Noth
characters.description: Howard Campbell's beloved German wife, who disappears during the Soviet advance near the end of WWII.

characters.name: Resi Noth
characters.description: Helga's younger sister, who resurfaces after the war impersonating Helga to get close to Howard Campbell.

characters.name: George Kraft
characters.description: Howard Campbell's friendly neighbor and chess partner in New York, who is secretly a Soviet spy named Ivan Yankovy.

characters.name: Frank Wirtanen
characters.description: The American intelligence officer who recruits Howard Campbell as a spy and holds the secret to his innocence.

characters.name: Senator Lister Rosewater
characters.description: Eliot Rosewater's conservative, patrician father, a US Senator who is appalled by his son's philanthropy.

characters.name: Sylvia Rosewater
characters.description: Eliot's aristocratic wife, who suffers a nervous breakdown from her husband's erratic generosity.

characters.name: Norman Mushari
characters.description: An ambitious young lawyer scheming to have Eliot declared insane in order to redirect the Rosewater fortune.

characters.name: Fred Rosewater
characters.description: A distant Rosewater cousin in Rhode Island whom Mushari hopes to make the new heir to the fortune.

characters.name: John (Jonah)
characters.description: The narrator, a writer researching what important Americans were doing on the day the atomic bomb was dropped.

characters.name: Frank Hoenikker
characters.description: Son of ice-nine's inventor, who becomes Minister of Science on San Lorenzo and offers John the presidency.

characters.name: Angela Hoenikker
characters.description: Felix Hoenikker's eldest daughter, who sacrificed her youth caring for her father and carries a chip of ice-nine.

characters.name: Newton Hoenikker
characters.description: Felix Hoenikker's dwarf son, who corresponds with John and unknowingly helps trigger the apocalypse.

characters.name: Mona Aamons Monzano
characters.description: The beautiful adopted daughter of San Lorenzo's dictator, a Bokononist saint and John's love interest.

characters.name: 'Papa' Monzano
characters.description: The dying dictator of San Lorenzo, who uses ice-nine to commit suicide, triggering a global catastrophe.

characters.name: Bokonon
characters.description: The founder of Bokononism, a religion built on 'foma' (comforting lies), officially outlawed but secretly tolerated on San Lorenzo.

characters.name: Malachi Constant
characters.description: The richest man in America, who is drawn into Winston Rumfoord's manipulations and ends up on Mars, Mercury, and Titan.

characters.name: Winston Niles Rumfoord
characters.description: A billionaire who flew his spaceship into a chrono-synclastic infundibulum, becoming a wave phenomenon spread across space.

characters.name: Beatrice Rumfoord
characters.description: Winston's haughty wife, who is manipulated by her husband's machinations into becoming Malachi's mate on Mars.

characters.name: Salo
characters.description: A Tralfamadorian robot stranded on Titan for millennia, waiting for a replacement part—the true cause of all human history.

characters.name: Paul Proteus
characters.description: A brilliant engineer and manager who grows disillusioned with the automated society he helps maintain.

characters.name: Anita Proteus
characters.description: Paul's ambitious wife, who married him for his status and becomes increasingly estranged as he rebels.

characters.name: Ed Finnerty
characters.description: Paul's old friend from engineering school, who has quit his prestigious job and become a rebel against the system.

characters.name: Rudy Waltz
characters.description: A pharmacist in Midland City who accidentally killed a pregnant woman with a rifle shot as a child, haunted by guilt.

characters.name: Felix Waltz
characters.description: Rudy's father, an admirer of Hitler and failed artist who befriended him in Vienna, leaving the family with a firearms collection.

characters.name: Walter F. Starbuck
characters.description: A Harvard-educated government official who served time for his peripheral role in Watergate.

characters.name: Mary Kathleen O'Looney
characters.description: Walter's college sweetheart who becomes a bag lady — and secretly the controlling shareholder of a massive corporation.

characters.name: Leon Trout
characters.description: Kilgore Trout's son, a ghost narrator who observes humanity's evolution over one million years from the Galápagos Islands.

characters.name: Mary Hepburn
characters.description: A widowed biology teacher who becomes the unlikely mother of humanity's next evolutionary branch on Santa Rosalia island.

characters.name: Monica Pepper
characters.description: Works at the Xanadu writers' retreat and is one of the people Kilgore Trout helps after the timequake's rerun ends.

characters.name: Kurt Vonnegut
characters.description: The author himself, who appears as a character in several of his own novels, blurring the line between fiction and autobiography.

interactions.description: Billy and Montana are placed together in a Tralfamadorian zoo, where they become companions and have a child.
Billy and Valencia are married. She dies rushing to see him in hospital after his plane crash.
Both are American POWs sheltered in Dresden's Slaughterhouse-Five during the firebombing.
Lazzaro blames Billy (at Roland Weary's dying request) for Weary's death and vows to have him killed.
Weary drags Billy through the snow during capture, blaming him when he dies of gangrene in a POW boxcar.
Lazzaro and Weary bond as fellow POWs; Weary's dying words instruct Lazzaro to avenge him against Billy.
Both are American POWs transported together in the same boxcar to Dresden.
Campbell appears in Dresden to recruit American POWs to fight for Germany; Billy witnesses this.
Billy discovers Trout's novels in a Times Square porn shop and becomes a devoted fan; they later meet at Billy's anniversary party.
Billy and Eliot share a ward in a veterans' hospital and bond over Kilgore Trout's science fiction novels.
Eliot introduces Billy to Trout's work while they are both recovering in the veterans' hospital.
Vonnegut inserts himself into the Dresden scenes, briefly appearing as a soldier among the POWs.
Dwayne reads Trout's novel 'Now It Can Be Told' and, taking it literally, attacks several people including Trout himself.
Dwayne and Francine are in a long-term affair; she is devoted to him despite his deteriorating mental state.
Dwayne is estranged from his gay son Bunny, who performs piano nearby but keeps emotional distance from his father.
Harry is Dwayne's loyal sales manager; Dwayne's breakdown puts their professional relationship in crisis.
Trout and Karabekian both attend the Midland City arts festival and interact in the hotel bar.
Vonnegut appears as a character who watches over Trout throughout the novel and finally meets him in the bar, granting him freedom.
Vonnegut observes Dwayne's unraveling throughout the novel as an authorial presence in Midland City.
Eliot is Trout's most devoted fan and has read everything Trout ever wrote; he summons Trout to Rosewater County.
Eliot and Sylvia are married, but his obsessive philanthropy drives her to a nervous breakdown and eventual separation.
The Senator is horrified by his son's behavior and is torn between familial love and embarrassment at Eliot's philanthropy.
Mushari plots to have Eliot declared insane in order to redirect the Rosewater fortune to a distant relative.
Eliot ultimately acknowledges all the children in Rosewater County as his own, thwarting Mushari's plan to install Fred as heir.
Mushari seeks out Fred as the intended beneficiary of his scheme to break the Rosewater Foundation's trust.
Howard and Helga are deeply in love; he calls their marriage a 'nation of two.' She disappears near the end of the war.
Resi impersonates Helga to get close to Howard; he falls in love with her before discovering she is a Soviet agent.
Howard and Kraft are close friends and chess partners in New York; Kraft is later revealed to be a Soviet spy.
Wirtanen recruits Howard as a spy and is the only person who can confirm Howard's innocence — but declines to testify.
Kraft and Resi are both Soviet agents working together in New York, using Howard Campbell for their own ends.
John travels to San Lorenzo and finds Frank, who offers him the presidency of the island in exchange for marrying Mona.
John interviews Angela as part of his research into her father Felix's life on the day of the atomic bomb.
Newt corresponds with John about his father Felix and later meets him in San Lorenzo.
John falls in love with Mona and agrees to become San Lorenzo's president partly to be with her.
John meets 'Papa' on his deathbed; Papa uses ice-nine to commit suicide, causing worldwide catastrophe.
Frank gave Papa a chip of ice-nine in exchange for his ministerial position and Mona's hand for John.
Frank was engaged to Mona before offering her to John as part of the presidential deal.
Bokonon and Papa co-founded the religion of Bokononism and its theatrical illegality to give the people of San Lorenzo meaning.
Rumfoord manipulates Malachi's entire life, having foreseen his destiny as part of a grand scheme culminating on Titan.
Malachi and Beatrice are manipulated into becoming mates on Mars; they have a son and eventually find peace on Titan.
Winston uses his foreknowledge to manipulate his own wife's life, sacrificing her happiness for his cosmic scheme.
Rumfoord and Salo become genuine friends on Titan; Rumfoord's entire scheme was unconsciously engineered by Tralfamadore to help Salo.
Salo befriends Malachi in his final years on Titan and, out of compassion, uses Tralfamadorian technology to give him a final vision of happiness.
Paul and Anita's marriage deteriorates as his disillusionment with the technocratic system grows and she clings to their status.
Ed reappears in Paul's life as a dropout from the system, encouraging Paul to question everything and eventually rebel.
Felix's firearms obsession gives Rudy access to the guns; the accidental shooting defines both their lives and legacies.
Kilgore Trout briefly appears in Midland City as part of the background of Rudy's world.
Walter and Mary Kathleen were college sweethearts separated by class and circumstance; he discovers she is secretly the world's most powerful businesswoman.
Trout makes a brief appearance as a fellow inmate or background figure in Walter Starbuck's post-prison world.
Leon's ghost narrates Mary's story across a million years; she becomes the unwitting matriarch of humanity's next form.
Kilgore Trout's ghost briefly appears to Leon, his son, offering him a chance to pass through the blue tunnel to the afterlife.
After the timequake's rerun ends and free will resumes, Trout rallies people including Monica with his cry 'You were sick, but now you're well.'
Vonnegut writes himself into Timequake as Trout's nephew-like companion, blurring the boundary between author and creation.
